title: Grade Calculator – Industrial Engineering Performance Score
description: Calculates an overall performance grade based on quality, throughput, cost, safety, and delivery metrics, aligned with ISO 9001, Lean, Six Sigma, and WERC standards.

inputs.label: Defect Rate (ppm)
inputs.businessContext: Parts per million defective – Six Sigma quality metric. Lower is better.

inputs.label: Throughput (units/hour)
inputs.businessContext: Actual production rate per hour. Used to calculate OEE and capacity utilization.

inputs.label: Planned Throughput (units/hour)
inputs.businessContext: Target production rate per hour. Must be >0 to avoid division by zero.

inputs.label: Cost per Unit ($)
inputs.businessContext: Direct manufacturing cost per unit. Includes material, labor, overhead.

inputs.label: Target Cost per Unit ($)
inputs.businessContext: Budgeted or standard cost per unit. Must be >0.

inputs.label: Safety Incidents (last 12 months)
inputs.businessContext: Total recordable incidents per year. Lower is better (ISO 45001).

inputs.label: On-Time Delivery (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of orders delivered on or before promised date. WERC metric.

inputs.label: Shift Type
inputs.businessContext: Shift pattern affects fatigue and error rates. Used in safety adjustment.

inputs.label: Override OEE Calculation?
inputs.businessContext: If true, user can manually enter OEE instead of computing from throughput.

inputs.label: Manual OEE (%)
inputs.businessContext: Overall Equipment Effectiveness if override is enabled.

rules.message: Throughput must be greater than zero.
Planned throughput must be greater than zero.
Target cost per unit must be greater than zero.

rules.condition: if oee_override == true then manual_oee >= 0 and manual_oee <= 100
rules.message: When OEE override is enabled, manual OEE must be between 0 and 100.
Defect rate must be between 0 and 100,000 ppm.

thresholds.message: Defect rate exceeds 10,000 ppm – immediate quality review recommended.
Cost per unit exceeds target by more than 20% – cost reduction action required.
Safety incidents exceed 5 per year – escalate to EHS team.
On-time delivery below 90% – investigate supply chain bottlenecks.

formulas.label: Quality Score (0-100)
formulas.description: Based on defect rate using Six Sigma scale. 100 = zero defects.

formulas.label: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (%)
formulas.description: If override is true, use manual OEE; else compute as throughput/planned_throughput * 100.
formulas.formula: if oee_override then manual_oee else (throughput / planned_throughput) * 100

formulas.label: Cost Efficiency Score (0-100)
formulas.description: Ratio of target cost to actual cost, capped at 100.

formulas.label: Safety Score (0-100)
formulas.description: Based on incident count. 0 incidents = 100, each incident reduces score by 10, with shift adjustment.

formulas.label: Delivery Score (0-100)
formulas.description: Direct mapping of on-time delivery percentage.

formulas.label: Weighted Grade (0-100)
formulas.description: Composite score using equal weights (20% each) for quality, OEE, cost, safety, delivery.

formulas.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Score
formulas.description: Reduces weighted grade by 5% if any input is at extreme (defect_rate > 50000 or safety_incidents > 20) to reflect lower data reliability.
formulas.formula: weighted_grade * (1 - 0.05 * (if defect_rate > 50000 or safety_incidents > 20 then 1 else 0))

primary.label: Overall Grade
primary.description: Final performance grade (A-F) based on data_confidence_adjusted score.
primary.value: if data_confidence_adjusted >= 90 then 'A' else if data_confidence_adjusted >= 80 then 'B' else if data_confidence_adjusted >= 70 then 'C' else if data_confidence_adjusted >= 60 then 'D' else 'F'

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Quality
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Defect rate is high, causing rework and scrap losses.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low OEE
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Equipment effectiveness is below target, indicating downtime or speed losses.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Cost
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Actual cost significantly exceeds target, eroding margins.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Safety Risk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Incident rate is high, leading to potential fines and lost workdays.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Delivery Failure
hiddenLossDrivers.description: On-time delivery below 80% causing customer dissatisfaction.

suggestedActions.action: Implement Six Sigma DMAIC project to reduce defect rate. Consider root cause analysis and SPC.
Conduct TPM (Total Productive Maintenance) and reduce changeover times using SMED.
Perform value stream mapping to identify waste. Target material and labor cost reduction.
Review safety protocols, increase training, and implement near-miss reporting per ISO 45001.
Analyze supply chain lead times, implement Kanban or pull system to improve on-time delivery.
